subject: Public Health
title: Groundwater and Arsenic Poisoning
description: A micro-lesson that makes Groundwater and Arsenic Poisoning usable.

hook.text: Bangladesh drilled millions of tube wells to escape surface water diseases—and found arsenic waiting underground.

buildup.text: Natural arsenic in geological formations dissolves into groundwater—invisible, tasteless, and deadly.

discovery.text: Chronic arsenic exposure causes skin lesions, cancers, cardiovascular disease, and cognitive impairment.

twist.text: Up to 77 million Bangladeshis were exposed—the WHO called it the largest mass poisoning in history.

climax.text: Testing wells, arsenic-removal filters, and piped water systems are gradually reducing exposure.

punchline.text: The solution to one water problem created another. Testing is essential.

quiz.question: What did the WHO call Bangladesh's arsenic crisis?
options: The largest mass poisoning in history
